rag_rules: AGREE assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes
When defining range constraints, use boolean helper declarations with explicit type alignment to avoid assumed/actual type mismatches
generic_rules: Use helper declarations for complex boolean expressions in AGREE contracts to improve readability and maintainability
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and their assigned expressions in AGREE contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of referenced ports
Boolean helper declarations should explicitly declare their type to prevent type mismatch errors
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for such elements
Do not mix assumed and actual types in helper declarations without explicit type alignment
